MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS New Delhi, the 18th September, 1987 NOTIFICATION G.S.R. 802(E).—In exercise of tho powers confer- red by section 87 of the Punjab Re-organisation Act. J 966 (31 of 1966), tht Central Government hereby extends to the Union Territory of Chandigarh, the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act, 1986 (Punjab Act No. 1 of 1987), as in force in the State of Punjab on the date of this notification, subject to the following modifications, namely :— MODIFICATIONS 1. In section I, for sub-section (2), the following sub-section shall be substituted, namely :— "(2) It shall come into force at once". 2. In section 3-B,— (i) In sub-section (1), for the words, brackets and figures -on and from the commence- ment of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act, 1986", the words "on and from the extension of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act, 1986 to the Union territory of Chandigarh" shall be substi- tuted; (ii) In the proviso, for the words, brackets and figures "before the commencement of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act 1986" tho words "before the extension of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act. 1986 to tho Union territory of Chandi- garh" shall bo substituted. 3. In section 3-C for the words "State of Punjab" wherever thoy occur the words "Union territory of Chandigarh" shall bo substituted. 4. Section 4 shall be omitted. -- 3 of 5 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC 3(0] ANNEXDRE THE PUNJAB MOTOR VEHICLES TAXATION (AMENDMENT) ACT, 1986- (Punjab Act No. 1 of 1987) AN ACT to amend the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation Act, 1924. BE it enacted by the Legislature of the State of Punjab in the Thirty-soventh Year of the Republic of India as follows :—• Short title and commencement. 1.(1) This Act may be called the Punjab Motor Vehiclos Taxation (Amendment) Act. 1986. (2) It shall come into force at once. Amendment of section 2 of Punjab Act 4 of 1924. 2. In the Punjab Motor Vehicle" Taxation Act, 1924 (hereinafter referrod to as the Principal Act), in section 2,— (i) the existing clause(a) shall be relcttercd as clause(aa) and the following clause shall be inserted before clausc(aa) as so roletterod, namely :— "(a) 'invalid carriage* means a motor vehicle the unladen weight whereof does not exceed 300 kilograms and which is spe- cially designed and completed, and not meiely adapted, for the use of a person suffering from some physical defect oi disability, and used solely by or for such a person"; (ii) after clause (aa) as so relettered, the following clause shall be inserted, namely :— "(aaa) 'motor cycle' means a two wheeled motor vehilcle the unladen weight whereof exclusive of the unladen weight of any detachable side car, having an extra wheel, attached to the motor vehicle, does not Dxceed 406.82 kilograms, but does not include an invalid carriage"; and (tii) after clause(c), the following clause shall be inserted, namely :—• "(cc) 'registration' means the registration of .T, motor vehicle under the provisions of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1939 (Central Act No. 4 of 1939)." Insertion of now sections 3-B and 3-C in Punjab Act 4 of 1924. 3. Tn the Principal Act, after section 3-A, the following sections shall be inserted, namely :— Imposition of tax in lump sum on motor cycle. ,"3-B(l) Notwithstanding anything contained in. section 3, on and from the extension of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act, 1986, to the Union territory of Chandigarh, tax on a motor cycle shall be leviable in Jump sum as hereinafter provided and the provisions of this Act shall apply mutatis mutandis to this modo of levy. (2) The rato of tax on a motor cycle having un- laden weight not exceeding 90.72 kilograms shall be one hundred & fifty rapeas and on other motor cycles it shall be five hundred rupees which shall be payable at the time of the registration of the motor vehicle : Provided that in the case of a motor cyclo registered before the extension of the Punjab Motor Vehicles Taxation (Amendment) Act, 1986 to the Union terri- tory ofChandigarh.it shall be optional for the person who keeps the motor cycle for use either to pay the lax in instalments as specifiod in section 3, or to pay the tax in lump sum as hereunder specified :— Si. Duration of use of motor No. cycle since the date of its registration I.- Less than three years 2. Three years or more but less than six years 3. Six years or mote but less than nine years 4. Nino years or more Rato of tax on a motor cycle having unladen weight not exceeding 90.72 kilograms (in rupees) Rate of tax on other motor cycles (in rupees) 120 400 90 300 60 200 30 10 -- 4 of 5 -- [fri II—WIT 3(i)] *frcr m <Hna : iTTTartur (3) The rate of tax specified in aub-soction (2) shall bo increased.by— (a) fifty per cent in respect of a motor cycle which is usod for drawing a Lroilor; and (b) twwity-fivo pei cent in respect of a motor cycle usod for drawing a side-car. Refund of tax in certain cases 3-C. Where an owner of a motor cycle in respect of which tax has been paid in lump sum under section 3-B ceases to be the resident of the Union teiritory of Chandigarh and takes alongwith him such motor cycle or if the ownership of the motor cycle is trans- ferred to a person having residence, outside the Union torritory of Chandigarh partial rofuud of the lump sum tax so paid shall be allowed as hereunder speci- fied. SI. Duration of use after No. registration of motor cycle. I • Less than throe years 2. Three years or more but less than six years. 3. Six years or more but less than nine years. i Rate of Rate of refund on refund on motor other mo- cycle tor cycles having (in rupees) unladen weight not exceeding 90.72 Kilograms (in rupees) 90 60 30 300 200 100 [(U-lI015/3/87-UTL)(17I)] LP, GUPTA, Addl.
